result:
**Goal: Add mouse interaction support — click to toggle/draw cells, scroll-wheel to zoom, and drag to pan the viewport.**

The project is an extraordinarily feature-rich terminal simulator with 130+ modes, but all interaction is keyboard-only. Adding mouse support via curses' built-in `mousemask()`/`getmouse()` (zero new dependencies) would be a major ergonomic leap — drawing patterns by clicking is far more intuitive than arrow-key cursor movement, scroll-wheel zoom replaces memorizing `+`/`-` keys, and click-drag panning makes navigating large grids natural. This single change makes every existing mode more accessible and pleasant to use.
